message:
feat(db,server,cli,installer): archive drift auto-recovery and duplicate canonical detection

Archive drift auto-recovery (startup_checks + pool):
- Startup integrity probe now compares SQLite message inventory against the
  canonical archive after a clean integrity check; if the archive is ahead
  (more unique message ids or a higher max id), it automatically triggers
  reconstruct_sqlite_file_with_archive_salvage before returning green.
- New pool::reconstruct_sqlite_file_with_archive_salvage quarantines the
  current primary DB, rebuilds from the archive with salvage, and restores
  the original if reconstruction fails.
- New query_database_message_inventory helper reads COUNT/MAX(id) from SQLite
  without going through the full pool.

Duplicate canonical message detection (reconstruct + cli):
- ReconstructStats now tracks duplicate_canonical_message_files and
  duplicate_canonical_message_ids; up to 5 individual warnings are emitted,
  then a summary warning when the count exceeds the sample limit.
- New ArchiveMessageInventory struct returned by scan_archive_message_inventory
  (lightweight read-only archive scan) exposes unique_message_ids,
  duplicate counts, latest_message_id, and parse_errors.
- Doctor archive inventory (DoctorArchiveInventory) tracks canonical file
  counts, duplicate files/ids, thread digests, and unparseable files.
  doctor_archive_db_drift_detail is now pure (no IO); callers collect
  inventory separately, enabling reuse without double-scanning.
- doctor_archive_inventory_suffix surfaces duplicate and thread-digest stats
  in the doctor check output.
- CPU sort in process sample summary uses total_cmp instead of partial_cmp.

Config env file lookup (core/config):
- user_env_file_candidates now always inserts ~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/ first
  before the XDG native config dir, so the portable installer path wins on
  macOS (where the native dir is ~/Library/Application Support/...).

Ammonia removal from TUI markdown (server/tui_markdown):
- Removed the ammonia-based TERMINAL_MARKDOWN_SANITIZER; ammonia is an HTML
  sanitizer that corrupts valid Markdown such as Rust generics (`Box<T>`),
  while offering no real protection since terminal renderers do not evaluate
  script tags. The ammonia_bug_test is updated to document the breakage.

Installer archive parity verification (install.sh):
- New installer_archive_parity_verify function runs `am doctor check --json`
  post-migration and fails the migration if the archive_db_parity check
  reports a failure, catching drift at install time across all recovery paths.

Clippy / formatting cleanup across all crates (no behaviour change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3483,11 +3483,7 @@ fn atc_runner_up_action(record: &atc::AtcDecisionRecord) -> Option<(String, f64)
         .loss_table
         .iter()
         .filter(|entry| entry.action != record.action)
-        .min_by(|left, right| {
-            left.expected_loss
-                .partial_cmp(&right.expected_loss)
-                .unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
-        })
+        .min_by(|left, right| left.expected_loss.total_cmp(&right.expected_loss))
         .map(|entry| (entry.action.clone(), entry.expected_loss))
 }
